title: An Account of Egypt
description: An Account of Egypt — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "An Account of Egypt" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Aegean Sea
memes.description: 'The sea between Greece and Asia Minor, mentioned by Herodotus in the context of Alexanders abduction of Helen and the Egyptian priests account of the Trojan War'

memes.label: Africa
memes.description: 'The continent known to Herodotus as Libya, lying west and south of Egypt and the Nile'

memes.label: Alexander (Paris)
memes.description: 'Trojan prince whose abduction of Helen triggered the Trojan War and who, in the Egyptian tradition, was tried and banished by King Proteus'

memes.label: Amasis
memes.description: 'Pharaoh of the 26th Dynasty who ruled Egypt during a period of great prosperity and cultivated strong ties with the Hellenic world'

memes.label: Ammon
memes.description: 'The Egyptian god Amun (Ammon), identified with Zeus by the Hellenes, whose oracle at Siwa was consulted by Herodotus and respected across the Mediterranean world.'

memes.label: Ammonians
memes.description: 'A Libyan people dwelling around the Oracle of Ammon, described by Herodotus as settlers of mixed Egyptian and Ethiopian origin'

memes.label: Ancient Greece
memes.description: 'The Hellenic world that serves as Herodotuss primary cultural reference point throughout his Egyptian account, constantly compared and contrasted with Egyptian customs'

memes.label: Animal Sacrifice
memes.description: 'The ritual slaughter and offering of animals to the gods in ancient Egypt as documented by Herodotus in the 5th century BCE'

memes.label: Anysis
memes.description: 'A blind Egyptian king of the Delta city Anysis, who hid from the Ethiopian king Sabacos for fifty years on an island in the Delta fens'

memes.label: Aphrodite
memes.description: 'Greek goddess of love, referenced by Herodotus in connection with Egyptian religious sites and practices.'

memes.label: Apollo
memes.description: 'The Greek god Apollo as referenced by Herodotus in the Egyptian context, identified with the Egyptian god Horus through interpretatio graeca'

memes.label: Apries
memes.description: 'Pharaoh of the 26th Dynasty overthrown by Amasis after a disastrous campaign against Cyrene'

memes.label: Arabia
memes.description: 'Region east of Egypt, source of stone for the pyramids'

memes.label: Ares
memes.description: 'Greek god of war, worshipped in Egypt at Papremis with a distinctive festival involving mock combat'

memes.label: Artemis
memes.description: 'The Greek goddess Artemis as identified by Herodotus with the Egyptian goddess Bastet, whosegreat festival at Bubastis was among the most celebrated in Egypt'

memes.label: Asychis
memes.description: "Egyptian king remembered for a unique law allowing borrowing against a deceased father's corpse"

memes.label: Atarbechis
memes.description: 'A city on the island of Prosopitis in the Nile Delta, home to a temple of Aphrodite and the origin point of the centralized cattle-bone reburial ritual described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Athene
memes.description: 'Greek goddess identified by Herodotus with the Egyptian goddess worshipped at Sais, where her great temple and festivals were held'

memes.label: Athens
memes.description: 'Greek city-state referenced by Herodotus in comparative geographic measurements and cultural observations about Egypt.'

memes.label: Branchidai
memes.description: 'Sanctuary and oracle of Apollo held by the Milesians, where Necos dedicated his war dress'

memes.label: Bubastis
memes.description: 'City in the Nile Delta sacred to the cat-goddess Bast, site of the most popular Egyptian religious festival'

memes.label: Burial
memes.description: 'Egyptian burial and mourning customs as described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Busiris
memes.description: 'Egyptian city in the central Delta, site of a great temple of Isis'

memes.label: Buto
memes.description: 'City in the Nile Delta housing the most truthful oracle of Leto in Egypt'

memes.label: Cadytis
memes.description: 'A great city of Syria captured by Necos II after his victory at Magdolos'

memes.label: Cambyses
memes.description: 'Persian king, son of Cyrus, who conquered Egypt'

memes.label: Canobus
memes.description: 'The westernmost navigable mouth of the Nile, gateway for Greek travelers to Egypt and site of a temple of Heracles, marking the northwestern limit of the Delta coastline'

memes.label: Carians
memes.description: "People from Caria in Asia Minor who served as mercenaries and settlers in Egypt, granted land by Psammetichos I"

memes.label: Cats in Ancient Egypt
memes.description: 'The sacred status of cats in ancient Egypt, their behaviour during fires, mourning customs upon their death, and embalming practices as recorded by Herodotus'

memes.label: Chemmis
memes.description: 'A large city in the Theban nome with a temple to Perseus, distinct from the floating island of Chemmis near Buto'

memes.label: Chemnis
memes.description: 'Mythical floating island near Buto where the goddess Leto hid the infant Apollo from Typhon'

memes.label: Cheops
memes.description: 'Fourth-dynasty pharaoh who built the Great Pyramid of Giza'

memes.label: Chephren
memes.description: 'An Egyptian king, brother of Cheops and successor to the throne, who built a pyramid on the plateau of Giza'

memes.label: Circumcision
memes.description: 'The Egyptian practice of circumcision as recorded by Herodotus'

memes.label: City of Crocodiles
memes.description: 'Egyptian city near the Labyrinth and Lake Moeris, center of the sacred crocodile cult'

memes.label: Colchians
memes.description: 'People of Colchis, described by Herodotus as descended from the Egyptian army of Sesostris and sharing customs with Egyptians.'

memes.label: Crocodile
memes.description: "Sacred and dangerous animal of the Nile, described in detail by Herodotus with its natural history and religious significance"

memes.label: Cyrene
memes.description: 'A Greek colony in Libya, referenced in Herodotus''s account as a landmark in the geography of the region bordering Egypt'

memes.label: Danae
memes.description: 'Mother of the Greek hero Perseus, referenced by Herodotus in connection with the Egyptian city of Chemmis.'

memes.label: Danaos
memes.description: 'Mythological figure of Egyptian origin, said by Herodotus to have sailed from Chemmis in Egypt to Hellas, and whose daughters introduced the Thesmophoria rites from Egypt to Greece.'

memes.label: Daphnai
memes.description: 'Egyptian frontier fortress in the district of Pelusion, guarding the eastern approach'

memes.label: Dareios
memes.description: 'The Persian king Dareios (Darius I), who appears in Herodotus''s account as the ruler who completed the canal from the Nile to the Erythraian Sea begun by Necos'

memes.label: Delphi
memes.description: 'The sanctuary of Apollo at Delphi, referenced by Herodotus in connection with Rhodopis dedication, the death of Aesop, and contributions from Pharaoh Amasis.'

memes.label: Demeter
memes.description: 'Greek goddess of agriculture and fertility, identified by Herodotus with the Egyptian goddess Isis.'

memes.label: Dionysos
memes.description: 'Greek god identified by Herodotus with the Egyptian Osiris'

memes.label: Divine Kingship in Egypt
memes.description: The integration of royal authority with religious belief in ancient Egypt as described by Herodotus in the 5th century BCE

memes.label: Dodona
memes.description: 'Ancient Greek oracle site in Epirus, whose prophetic traditions Herodotus connects to Egyptian origins'

memes.label: Egypt
memes.description: 'The land of Egypt as described by Herodotus — its geography, boundaries, the Nile, and the people who called it home'

memes.label: Egyptian Agriculture
memes.description: 'The agricultural practices of ancient Egypt as described by Herodotus, characterized by the ease of cultivation made possible by the annual Nile inundation'

memes.label: Egyptian Altars
memes.description: 'Altars and sacrificial practices in ancient Egyptian religion as recorded by Herodotus'

memes.label: Egyptian Beverages
memes.description: 'Fermented and non-fermented beverages consumed in ancient Egypt as described by Herodotus, including barley-beer and grape-wine'

memes.label: Egyptian Boats
memes.description: 'The construction and use of boats and ships in ancient Egypt as described by Herodotus, including the acacia-built baris cargo vessel, Nile navigation practices, the Elephantine passage, and the monolith transport of Amasis'

memes.description: 'The production and ritual significance of bread in ancient Egypt, including sacred bread baked for the priesthood, the maize-based kyllestis loaf, and bread as a staple of military rations'

memes.description: 'The clothing, garments, and dress customs of ancient Egyptians as described by Herodotus, including linen garments, wool usage, priestly attire, cleanliness rites, and mourning dress'

memes.description: 'Island city at the southern frontier of Egypt, marking the boundary between Egypt and Ethiopia'

memes.label: Embalming
memes.description: 'Egyptian mortuary practice of preserving the dead through evisceration desiccation and wrapping as described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Epaphos
memes.description: 'Egyptian deity associated with the sacred bull, identified by Herodotus as the Egyptian counterpart of the Greek Epaphos, to whom male oxen were considered to belong'

memes.label: Erythraian Sea
memes.description: 'The sea to the east of Egypt, known as the Arabian Gulf or Red Sea, linked by an attempted canal under Necos and completed under Darius'

memes.label: Ethiopia
memes.description: 'The kingdom south of Egypt, source of the Nile and homeland of the 25th Dynasty conquerors of Egypt'

memes.label: Feast
memes.description: 'Egyptian feasting customs and festive meals as recorded by Herodotus'

memes.label: Featured Articles
memes.description: 'A curated list of featured and premium content across all entity types in "An Account of Egypt"'

memes.label: Gift of the River
memes.description: 'Herodotus''s famous characterization of Egypt as a "gift of the river" — the idea that Lower Egypt was formed over time by Nile silt deposits, making the habitable land a creation of the river itself'
aliases: Egypt as gift of the Nile

memes.label: Gulf of Suez
memes.description: 'The northwestern arm of the Red Sea, called the Arabian Gulf by Herodotus and linked to the Nile by Necos''s canal'

memes.label: Helen
memes.description: "Daughter of Tyndareus and wife of Menelaos, whose abduction by Paris and stay in Egypt form a central episode in Herodotus's account of the Trojan War"

memes.label: Helen of Troy
memes.description: 'Spartan queen whose abduction by Alexander (Paris) triggered the Trojan War and who, according to Egyptian priests, was detained in Egypt by King Proteus'

memes.label: Heliopolis
memes.description: 'Religious centre of the sun-cult near Memphis, home to the most learned Egyptian priests'

memes.label: Hellas
memes.description: 'The Greek homeland and cultural world, constantly contrasted with Egypt throughout Herodotus\'s account as the lens through which Egyptian customs, geography, and history are interpreted'

memes.label: Hellenes
memes.description: 'The Greek people as described by Herodotus in his Egyptian account, serving as a constant comparative frame for Egyptian customs and achievements.'

memes.label: Hephaistos
memes.description: 'The Egyptian god identified by Herodotus with the Greek Hephaistos, whose great temple at Memphis was one of the most important religious monuments of Egypt'

memes.label: Heracles
memes.description: 'The ancient Egyptian god whom Herodotus identifies with the Greek Heracles — one of the twelve gods, distinct from the Hellenic hero'

memes.label: Herodotus
memes.description: 'Greek historian from Halicarnassus whose Histories Book II provides the source text for this wiki'

memes.label: Homer
memes.description: 'The Greek epic poet whose knowledge of the Egyptian Helen tradition Herodotus argues from internal evidence in the Iliad'

memes.label: Homeric
memes.description: 'References to Homer and the Homeric tradition in the Histories of Herodotus — his critical engagement with the Iliad, Odyssey, and Cyprian Epic in constructing Egyptian history'

memes.label: Horus
memes.description: 'Major Egyptian deity, identified by Herodotus with Apollo, whose principal oracle was at Buto in the Nile Delta'

memes.label: Ichneumon
memes.description: 'The ichneumon, or Egyptian mongoose, one of the animals held sacred by the ancient Egyptians and buried in sacred tombs alongside dogs, as described by Herodotus in his survey of Egyptian animal worship'

memes.label: Ilion
memes.description: 'The city of Troy, referenced by Herodotus in the context of the Egyptian priests alternative account of the Trojan War, in which Helen never reached Ilion but remained in Egypt.'

memes.label: Immortality of the Soul
memes.description: 'The Egyptian doctrine of the souls immortality and its transmigration through living creatures, as reported by Herodotus'

memes.label: India
memes.description: 'Distant eastern land mentioned by Herodotus in connection with trade routes linking Egypt to the Erythraian Sea and beyond'

memes.label: Ionia
memes.description: 'Region on the western coast of Anatolia, home to Greek Ionian cities referenced by Herodotus in his account of Egypt.'

memes.label: Ionians
memes.description: 'The Ionian Greeks, inhabitants of the central Aegean coast of Anatolia and the islands, as they appear in the Account of Egypt as traders, mercenaries, and cultural intermediaries'

memes.label: Isis
memes.description: 'The Egyptian goddess Isis, universally revered across Egypt, identified with Demeter by Herodotus and central to the Osiris myth recounted in the Account'

memes.label: Isis and Osiris
memes.description: 'The two Egyptian deities universally worshipped by all Egyptians — Isis (identified with Demeter) and Osiris (identified with Dionysos) — central to Egyptian religion in Herodotus''s account'

memes.label: Kings of Egypt
memes.description: 'Catalogue of Egyptian kings and rulers mentioned by Herodotus in the Account of Egypt'

memes.label: Kyrenian
memes.description: 'The people and culture of Kyrene, a Greek colony in Libya mentioned by Herodotus as informants and political allies of Egypt'

memes.label: Labyrinth
memes.description: 'The vast Egyptian labyrinth near Lake Moeris, which Herodotus personally visited and described as surpassing all Greek works combined.'

memes.label: Ladike
memes.description: 'Greek wife of king Amasis, central figure in a story of divine intervention'

memes.label: Lake Moeris
memes.description: 'Artificial lake in the Fayum basin, a marvel of Egyptian hydraulic engineering admired by Herodotus'

memes.label: Leto
memes.description: 'One of the eight primordial Egyptian gods, whose temple at Buto housed the floating island of Chemmis where Isis hid the infant Apollo from Typhon'

memes.label: Libya
memes.description: "Region west of Egypt, one of the three continents recognized by Herodotus alongside Asia and Europe"

memes.label: Libyan Desert
memes.description: 'Vast desert region west of the Nile, part of the larger Libyan landmass described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Libyans
memes.description: 'The peoples inhabiting the region west of Egypt, referenced by Herodotus throughout his account for comparative ethnography and geographical context.'

memes.label: Lotos
memes.description: 'The aquatic lily plant of the Nile that the Egyptians harvested for food, as described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Magdolos
memes.description: 'Site of a battle where the Egyptian king Necos II defeated the Syrians, after which he captured the city of Cadytis'

memes.label: Marea
memes.description: 'Egyptian city on the Libyan border, site of a Persian garrison'

memes.label: Mediterranean Sea
memes.description: 'The sea connecting Egypt to the Hellenic world, Phoenicia, and the wider Mediterranean basin'

memes.label: Memphis
memes.description: 'Ancient capital of Egypt, located at the apex of the Nile Delta, central to Egyptian religion and kingship'

memes.label: Mendes
memes.description: 'A city and district of the Nile Delta whose inhabitants abstained from goats and sacrificed sheep, revering the god Pan (Mendes) above all others'

memes.label: Menelaos
memes.description: 'The Spartan king and husband of Helen, whose journey to Egypt to recover her from Proteus is a central episode in Herodotus''s alternative Trojan War tradition'

memes.label: Min
memes.description: 'The first king of Egypt (Menes), who founded Memphis and the temple of Hephaistos, and the Egyptian god identified by Herodotus with Pan — a dual figure in the historian''s account'

memes.label: Moiris
memes.description: 'Egyptian king who constructed the lake of Moiris and the gateway of the temple of Hephaistos'

memes.label: Momemphis
memes.description: 'The city of Momemphis in the Egyptian Delta, site of the decisive battle between Apries and Amasis that ended the 26th Dynasty civil war'

memes.label: Mycerinus
memes.description: 'Pharaoh of the 4th Dynasty, builder of the third pyramid at Giza, known in Herodotus for his just reign and the misattribution of his pyramid to Rhodopis'

memes.label: Mykerinos
memes.description: 'Son of Cheops, known for his just rule and smaller pyramid at Giza'

memes.label: Nasamonians
memes.description: 'A Libyan tribe dwelling in the Syrtis region, known to Herodotus for their legendary expedition across the Sahara in search of the Nile sources'

memes.label: Naucratis
memes.description: 'Greek trading settlement in the Nile Delta, granted by Amasis as a commercial port for Hellenic merchants'

memes.label: Neco
memes.description: 'Egyptian pharaoh of the 26th Dynasty, son of Psammetichos, builder of the canal to the Red Sea'

memes.label: Necos
memes.description: 'Pharaoh of the 26th Dynasty who attempted a canal to the Red Sea and sent a Phoenician circumnavigation of Africa'

memes.label: Nile Delta
memes.description: 'The triangular alluvial plain at the mouth of the Nile, comprising Lower Egypt and its five ancient mouths'

memes.label: Nile Delta Region
memes.description: 'A dynamic index of cities, sites, and features of the Nile Delta region documented by Herodotus'

memes.label: Nile Flood
memes.description: 'The annual inundation of the Nile River observed by Herodotus in 5th-century BCE Egypt'

memes.label: Nile Geography
memes.description: 'A dynamic index of places and concepts related to the Nile River and its delta, as described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Nile Mouths
memes.description: 'The seven mouths of the Nile as enumerated by Herodotus, five of which were navigable'

memes.label: Nile River
memes.description: 'The great river of Egypt, central to Herodotus''s account — its geography, annual flood, Delta, and seven mouths'

memes.label: Oracle of Buto
memes.description: 'The oracle of the goddess Leto at the city of Buto in Egypt — the most honoured oracle among the Egyptians, featuring a floating island temple and a gateway of remarkable height'

memes.label: Oracles in Egypt
memes.description: 'The oracular institutions of ancient Egypt as described by Herodotus, centered on the great oracle of Leto at Buto, where prophecies shaped royal decisions and personal fates'

memes.label: Osiris
memes.description: 'The Egyptian god Osiris, identified with Dionysos by Herodotus and universally revered across Egypt alongside Isis, whose son Horus was pursued by Typhon'

memes.label: Papremis
memes.description: 'A city in the Nile Delta where the festival of Ares was celebrated with a ritual mock battle and where the hippopotamus was held sacred'

memes.label: Papyrus
memes.description: 'The papyrus plant as described by Herodotus, its uses in Egyptian daily life including food, boat-building, writing material, and priestly equipment'

memes.label: Patarbemis
memes.description: 'A distinguished Egyptian courtier under King Apries, whose mutilation by the king triggered the final defection of the Egyptian nobility to Amasis during the civil war of the 26th Dynasty'

memes.label: Pelusion
memes.description: 'Strategic fortress city at the easternmost mouth of the Nile, gateway between Egypt and Asia'

memes.label: People of Egypt
memes.description: 'A comprehensive index of all historical and mythological figures of ancient Egypt documented in this wiki'

memes.label: Perseus
memes.description: 'Greek hero worshipped at Chemmis in Egypt, claimed by both Egyptians and Hellenes; his cult included athletic games unique in Egypt'

memes.label: Persian
memes.description: 'The Persian Empire under the Achaemenid dynasty, which conquered Egypt and figures prominently in Herodotus as both antagonist and administrative successor.'

memes.label: Pheros
memes.description: 'Egyptian king, son of Sesostris, struck blind for impiety'

memes.label: Phoenicia
memes.description: 'Coastal region of the Levant whose seafaring people appear throughout Herodotus''s account of Egypt'

memes.label: Phoenicians
memes.description: 'A seafaring people of the Levantine coast, mentioned by Herodotus as traders, colonizers, and temple-founders connected with Egypt.'

memes.label: Phoenix
memes.description: 'The sacred bird of Heliopolis, said by Herodotus to appear every five hundred years, described as having gold and red plumage and resembling an eagle in size and form'

memes.label: Pillars of Heracles
memes.description: 'The Pillars of Heracles, marking the western limit of the known world at the Strait of Gibraltar, and the commemorative pillars erected by Sesostris on his campaigns'

memes.label: Places of Egypt
memes.description: 'A comprehensive index of all cities, temples, regions, and natural features of Egypt documented in this wiki'

memes.label: Polycrates
memes.description: 'Tyrant of Samos and guest-friend of king Amasis'

memes.label: Priam
memes.description: 'King of Troy, mentioned by Herodotus in defense of the Trojans regarding Helen'

memes.label: Prosopitis
memes.description: 'An island in the Nile Delta, home to the city of Atarbechis with its temple of Aphrodite, from which boats were sent to collect and rebury the bones of sacrificial oxen across Egypt'

memes.label: Proteus
memes.description: 'Egyptian king of Memphis in Herodotus who sheltered Helen of Troy during the Trojan War'

memes.label: Psammetichos
memes.description: 'First pharaoh of the 26th Dynasty who unified Egypt with the help of Ionian and Carian mercenaries'

memes.label: Psammetichos I
memes.description: 'The first pharaoh of the Twenty-sixth Dynasty, who reunified Egypt from the twelve kings, conducted the famous language experiment, and ruled for fifty-four years'

memes.label: Psammis
memes.description: 'Egyptian king, son of Psammetichos, who ruled for six years'

memes.label: Pyramids
memes.description: 'The great pyramids of Egypt, described by Herodotus as marvels surpassing all Greek works combined'

memes.label: Pyramids of Giza
memes.description: 'The three great pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinos at Giza, among the most famous monuments of the ancient world'

memes.label: Religious Purification
memes.description: 'Egyptian religious practices of purification and ritual cleansing described by Herodotus, including temple protocols, priestly standards, and bodily purity customs'

memes.label: Rhampsinitos
memes.description: 'Egyptian king of vast wealth known for the story of the clever thief and his descent to the underworld'

memes.label: Rhodopis
memes.description: "Greek courtesan in Egypt during the reign of Amasis, famous for her beauty and wealth, mistakenly credited by some Greeks with building a pyramid"

memes.label: Ritual Purity
memes.description: 'The system of bodily ablutions garment regulations dietary restrictions and sacrificial protocols governing ritual cleanliness in ancient Egyptian religion as recorded by Herodotus'

memes.label: Sabacos
memes.description: 'Ethiopian king who conquered Egypt and ruled during the 25th Dynasty'

memes.label: Sacred Animals of Egypt
memes.description: 'The Egyptian practice of venerating certain animals as sacred, including crocodiles, cats, ibises, and other creatures'

memes.label: Sacred Animals of Egypt
memes.description: 'A dynamic index of sacred animals and animal cults in ancient Egypt documented by Herodotus'

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: 'Egyptian sacrificial practices as observed and described by Herodotus'

memes.label: Sais
memes.description: 'City in the western Nile Delta, cult centre of Neith/Athene and seat of the 26th Dynasty pharaohs'

memes.label: Sappho
memes.description: 'Greek lyric poet of Lesbos, sister of Charaxos who redeemed Rhodopis from slavery in Egypt; mentioned by Herodotus for her abuse of Charaxos in verse'

memes.label: Saqqara
memes.description: 'The necropolis of ancient Memphis, later famous for the Serapeum where the Apis bulls were entombed — a tradition whose origins Herodotus encountered in the living Apis cult at Memphis'

memes.label: "Section 1"
memes.description: "Bibliographic information, editor's NOTE on Herodotus, and the beginning of the account of Egypt"

memes.label: "Section 2"
memes.description: "The geography of Egypt and the formation of the Nile Delta"

memes.label: "Section 3"
memes.description: "Theories on the causes of the Nile flood"

memes.label: "Section 4"
memes.description: "The source of the Nile, the boundaries of Egypt, and explorations"

memes.label: "Section 5"
memes.description: "Egyptian customs and religion (Part 1)"

memes.label: "Section 6"
memes.description: "Egyptian customs and religion (Part 2)"

memes.label: "Section 7"
memes.description: "Gods, sacrifices, and animal worship"

memes.label: "Section 8"
memes.description: "Nile animals, crocodiles, and the phoenix"

memes.label: "Section 9"
memes.description: "Burial, embalming, and daily customs"

memes.label: "Section 10"
memes.description: "Egyptian kings: Min to Moiris"

memes.label: "Section 11"
memes.description: "The conquests and achievements of Sesostris"

memes.label: "Section 12"
memes.description: "The succession of kings and the building of the pyramids"

memes.label: "Section 13"
memes.description: "Cheops, Chephren, Mycerinus"

memes.label: "Section 14"
memes.description: "Kings continued, the Labyrinth, and Lake Moeris"

memes.label: "Section 15"
memes.description: "Sethos, the twelve kings, and Psammetichus"

memes.label: "Section 16"
memes.description: "Necos, Apries, and Amasis (Part 1)"

memes.label: "Section 17"
memes.description: "The reign of Amasis and conclusion (Cyprus)"

memes.label: Sesostris
memes.description: Legendary Egyptian pharaoh and conqueror who subdued nations across Asia, Europe, and Ethiopia and transformed Egypt's landscape with canals

memes.label: Sethos
memes.description: 'Priest of Hephaistos who became king of Egypt and miraculously repelled an Assyrian invasion with divine aid'

memes.label: Sidon
memes.description: 'The Phoenician city of Sidon, referenced by Herodotus as a destination of Alexanders wanderings in Homers account and a target of Egyptian military campaigns'

memes.label: Siwa Oasis
memes.description: 'Remote desert oasis in the Libyan Desert, site of the renowned Oracle of Ammon'

memes.label: Sparta
memes.description: 'Greek city-state in Laconia, home of Helen and Menelaos, mentioned by Herodotus in his argument about the true story of the Trojan War'

memes.label: Syria
memes.description: 'The region bordering Egypt to the northeast, whose inhabitants Herodotus describes as having adopted circumcision and other practices from the Egyptians.'

memes.label: Taricheiai
memes.description: 'Egyptian settlement at the Canobic mouth of the Nile where Alexander (Paris) landed with Helen'

memes.label: Temple of Hephaistos
memes.description: 'The great temple of Hephaistos (Ptah) at Memphis, one of the most important religious monuments of Egypt, featuring gateways built by successive kings and the statues of Summer and Winter'

memes.label: The Twelve Kings
memes.description: 'The period of twelve simultaneous kings who ruled Egypt after the reign of the priest of Hephaistos, as recorded by Herodotus'

memes.label: The World Beyond Egypt
memes.description: 'A dynamic index of people, places, and events outside Egypt mentioned by Herodotus in his account'

memes.label: Thebes
memes.description: 'Great city of Upper Egypt, religious capital and seat of the god Ammon, known to the Greeks as Diospolis'

memes.label: Thonis
memes.description: 'Egyptian warden of the Nile river-mouth who detained Alexander (Paris)'

memes.label: Trojan War
memes.description: 'Legendary conflict between Greeks and Trojans, whose historicity Herodotus examines through Egyptian priestly sources'

memes.label: Troy
memes.description: 'The city of Troy (Ilion), whose siege by the Hellenes forms the backdrop to Herodotus''s alternative Egyptian tradition of the Helen story'

memes.label: Typhon
memes.description: 'The Egyptian deity defeated by Oros (Apollo), associated with the Osiris myth in Herodotus.'

memes.label: Tyre
memes.description: 'Phoenician city visited by Herodotus, known for its temple of Heracles'

memes.label: Upper Egypt
memes.description: 'The southern region of Egypt, centered on Thebes, which Herodotus distinguished from the Delta and described as the historic core of Egyptian civilization — the part of the country that, unlike the silt-formed Delta, was not a "gift of the river"'

memes.label: Wadi Tumilat
memes.description: 'The valley east of the Nile Delta through which the canal connecting the Nile to the Erythraian Sea was cut, begun by Necos and completed under Dareios'

memes.label: Zeus
memes.description: 'The chief Hellenic deity, identified by Herodotus with the Egyptian Amun, whose oracles and temples in Egypt and Libya feature in his account.'
